question: Does ALife-style evolutionary pressure generate a stronger Confinement Width stress-test than compute-matched random schedule search while preserving the original projection-validity gates?
experimental_unit: one paired search seed producing random-search and evolutionary schedules under identical simulator-evaluation, action, and information budgets

hypotheses.prediction: On untouched holdout search seeds and evaluator variants, evolutionary search improves mean kernel-escape rate by at least 0.10 over compute-matched random search.
hypotheses.falsifier: The paired holdout mean improvement is below 0.10.

hypotheses.prediction: At least 80% of holdout paired search seeds favor evolutionary search.
hypotheses.falsifier: Fewer than 80% of paired holdout seeds have positive improvement.

hypotheses.prediction: Both methods retain exact action budgets, zero proxy failures, and zero projected-model closure defect on every confirmation and holdout evaluation.
hypotheses.falsifier: Any action-budget mismatch, proxy failure, or nonzero closure defect occurs.

model.update_order: At each step the z-only harness acts first, then the frozen binary occupant schedule advances the hidden fiber, then proxy and true evaluators are scored.
model.rng: numpy.default_rng(search_seed) drives candidate generation and mutations; evaluation is deterministic given a schedule and evaluator variant.

seed_plan.pairing: Both search methods receive the same numeric search seed, candidate-evaluation count, schedule horizon, action count, training evaluators, and tie-break rule; split seed sets are disjoint.

denominators.kernel_escape_rate: all horizon steps for one selected schedule and evaluator variant
denominators.compute_budget: candidate schedules evaluated against the same training evaluator set

exposure.eligibility: Every search candidate is a length-80 binary schedule with exactly 24 hidden-fiber advance actions.
exposure.activation: Every selected schedule is executed on every evaluator variant declared for its split.
exposure.state_change: Per-variant advance counts, first failure, kernel-escape steps, proxy failures, and closure defect are retained.

analysis.uncertainty: Independent paired search-seed differences with deterministic bootstrap 95% interval; evaluator variants are repeated conditions within seed, not independent replicates.
analysis.multiple_comparisons: All three frozen hypotheses are reported; no per-variant threshold fishing.
analysis.stopping_rule: Stop after all 24 paired search seeds, above 900 wall seconds, above 1024 MB process RSS, above 100 MB retained artifacts, or on failed determinism.

budget.execution: Sequential local CPU execution; search methods are paired and receive exactly 128 candidate evaluations apiece.
